page: Microsoft Azure DC32eds_v6 is an x86_64 architecture server instance belonging to the DCEDV6 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 32 dedicated Intel vCPUs and 128.0 GB of system memory, yielding an allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. The instance comes equipped with 1890 GB of local storage and is capable of utilizing Premium Storage. It does not include any GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

From a resource density perspective, the instance provides a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio alongside a substantial local storage allocation. While designated within a memory-optimized family, the hardware configuration aligns closely with general-purpose and storage-heavy workloads due to the integrated 1890 GB local disk. The lack of integrated GPU hardware limits its utility for accelerated computing tasks, and the absence of complimentary public IPv4 addresses requires external networking configurations for public internet access. The dedicated CPU allocation ensures consistent processing capabilities without resource sharing.

This server configuration is suitable for database deployments, enterprise applications requiring local scratch space, and general-purpose workloads that benefit from dedicated CPU resources and fast local storage.
description: Microsoft Azure DC32eds_v6 is an x86_64 architecture server instance from the DCEDV6 family, operating under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It is configured with 32 dedicated Intel vCPUs and 128.0 GB of system memory, providing a ratio of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. The instance features 1890 GB of local storage and supports Premium Storage, though it lacks integrated GPUs and complimentary public IPv4 addresses. Despite its family designation, the 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and significant local storage capacity make it highly suitable for database hosting, data-intensive applications, and general-purpose enterprise workloads requiring dedicated CPU allocation and substantial local disk space. The absence of public IPv4 addresses requires external networking configurations for public internet access, while the dedicated CPU allocation ensures consistent processing capabilities without resource sharing.
